nativeRange.habitat: Compact hobby cultivar derived from Bucephalandra collected on Borneo, where the genus grows as a rheophyte on rocks and submerged wood in fast-flowing rainforest streams that periodically expose and resubmerge the plants

decorPreferences: lava rock, driftwood, or porous hardscape for attaching the small rhizome
moderate flow across the rhizome to mimic stream conditions
shaded to moderately lit foreground or midground placement; well suited to nano scapes
stable, established tank — avoid introducing into newly cycled systems

goodWith: shrimp (Neocaridina and Caridina) that graze biofilm without harming the leaves
small peaceful community fish suited to nano tanks
other low-light epiphytes such as Anubias nana petite, Java moss and mini ferns
avoidWith: large herbivorous or rhizome-disturbing fish such as goldfish, silver dollars and large plecos
vigorous diggers (large cichlids) that may dislodge the small rhizome
newly set up tanks with unstable parameters

summary: A compact dwarf cultivar of Bucephalandra with small, wavy green-to-bluish-purple leaves. Treated like the genus — rhizome attached to rock or wood, never buried — but its small footprint makes it especially well suited to nano tanks and foreground placement.
careNotes: Bucephalandra 'Mini Boyan' is a small, slow-growing cultivar of the Borneo-endemic aroid genus Bucephalandra (family Araceae). Husbandry is the same as for the parent genus entry (fw-plant-016): the creeping rhizome is attached to lava rock, driftwood or other porous hardscape with super glue gel, sewing thread or fishing line and is left exposed to the water column — burying the rhizome in substrate is generally reported to cause it to rot. Reported water-parameter ranges across hobby sources cluster around 21-28 °C and pH ~6.0-7.5 (Modern Aquarium and Aquarium Co-Op both cite 70-82 °F / 21-28 °C; Aquarium Co-Op extends pH to 6-8). Lighting needs are flexible: low light is sufficient for survival, while medium light brings out the cultivar's characteristic green-to-bluish-purple iridescence; high light without CO2 tends to trigger algae on the slow-growing leaves. CO2 is optional but produces visibly better growth and color. Because the plant feeds from the water column, substrate type is irrelevant and root tabs are not needed; modest liquid fertilization (an all-in-one product such as Easy Green) is sufficient — Modern Aquarium notes that moderate fertilization helps bring out coloration in this cultivar specifically. Growth is very slow (commonly one new leaf every 2-4 weeks). 'Mini Boyan' is one of the smaller cultivars: Modern Aquarium describes 1-2 inch (~3-5 cm) stem lengths and a tendency to form dense, bushy clusters over time, so a mature clump is typically around 3-8 cm tall and somewhat wider. This compact habit makes it a strong choice for foreground or midground placement in nano and aquascape tanks. As with all Bucephalandra, expect 'buce melt' on freshly imported emersed-grown plants and after sudden parameter shifts; as long as the rhizome stays firm, recovery from the rhizome is generally reported within a few weeks. Introduce only into stable, established tanks. Source note: the originally specified secondary (Buce Plant product page) returned 404; cross-verification used Aquarium Co-Op's Bucephalandra care article from the fallback chain.
breedingNotes: Propagated by rhizome division. Once the plant has established and put out new leaves and roots, cut the rhizome at a natural bend or constriction with clean sharp scissors, leaving each division with several leaves and visible roots, and reattach the new section to a separate piece of hardscape. With 'Mini Boyan' the rhizome is small, so divisions of even 1-2 cm with at least a couple of leaves are commonly viable. Modern Aquarium notes the cultivar naturally forms dense clusters over time, which can simply be teased apart and replanted.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Primary source. Care page specifically for the 'Mini Boyan' cultivar: Borneo origin, low-to-moderate light, slow growth, foreground/midground epiphyte placement, must not be buried, moderate fertilization recommended, propagation by rhizome division forming dense clusters, 70-82 °F (21-28 °C), 1-2 inch stem lengths, suitable for nano tanks.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: Fallback secondary after the assigned Buce Plant product page returned 404. Genus-level Bucephalandra care article used to cross-verify Borneo rheophyte origin, 70-82 °F / 21-28 °C, pH 6-8, low-to-medium light, optional CO2, foreground/midground/hardscape placement, no substrate needed, propagation by cutting the rhizome, beginner-friendly care level, and suitability for nano tanks.
